topic.theme: AI should replace most customer service jobs
topic.pro_position: AI should replace most customer service jobs
topic.con_position: AI should not replace most customer service jobs

events.content:
Theme: AI should replace most customer service jobs
Pro: AI should replace most customer service jobs
Con: AI should not replace most customer service jobs
AI can absorb repetitive customer service work faster, cheaper, and around the clock, freeing people to handle complex exceptions and relationship building.
Customer service is not only ticket resolution; it also handles fragile emotions, edge cases, and trust repair where human judgment matters.

scores.pro_analysis: The pro side established a clean efficiency case.
scores.con_analysis: The con side identified hidden assumptions about service quality.
key_moments: The distinction between repetitive work and trust repair.
scores.reasoning: Con addressed the missing human edge more directly.

final.verdict_document: Con wins on stronger engagement with service quality and edge cases.

summary.content: The debate turns on whether customer service is primarily a process-optimization problem or a trust-and-judgment problem. A realistic takeaway is selective automation rather than blanket replacement.
